source.note: Internal provenance metadata for climate verification; not rendered in the UI.

desc: Antigua is a beautifully preserved highland colonial city ringed by volcanoes, coffee farms, and terraced hills. It combines UNESCO architecture and a strong café/remote-work scene with easy access to volcano hikes and local craft villages, making it one of Central America's strongest culture-plus-adventure short breaks.
hls: Strong cafe and digital nomad scene

todo.name: Santa Catalina Arch
todo.desc: The iconic yellow arch and surrounding streets are Antigua's postcard view, especially at sunrise with Volcán de Agua in the background.

todo.name: Iglesia de La Merced
todo.desc: A richly ornamented baroque church with one of the city's most striking facades and photogenic courtyards.

todo.name: Cerro de la Cruz viewpoint
todo.desc: A short uphill walk to panoramic views across Antigua's rooftops to the surrounding volcanoes.

todo.name: Volcán Pacaya hike
todo.desc: Popular half-day excursion to active volcanic terrain with lava fields and wide valley views.

todo.name: Coffee farm tour
todo.desc: Learn the full bean-to-cup process in one of Guatemala's best-known coffee regions.

todo.name: Local markets & artisan workshops
todo.desc: Browse textiles, ceramics, and handcrafted goods in and around the old town.

wifi.note: Generally reliable in central hotels and cafés, with multiple coworking-friendly venues and good mobile-data fallback.

prac.fltNote: No direct London departures; UK itineraries usually connect into Guatemala City (GUA) via Madrid, Amsterdam, Mexico City, or major US hubs. Return fares are often ~£480-£760 off-peak, ~£760-£980 in shoulder demand, and ~£980+ around Christmas/Easter peaks.
prac.visa: UK and US passport holders can generally enter Guatemala visa-free for short tourist stays (commonly up to 90 days; confirm latest policy before travel).
alerts: Altitude is around 1,500 m; stay hydrated for your first day.
Road transfer times from Guatemala City can vary significantly with traffic and weather.
Wet-season afternoons (roughly May-October) often bring heavy rain — schedule volcano hikes early.

sweet: November-April is usually best for drier skies and clearer volcano views, while shoulder wet-season months can offer lower pricing.
